commands.author: ForceConstant

target.title: fix(agent): classify stuck recovery as idle timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Fixes #93610. opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash runs that stopped making model-stream progress were recovered by the stuck-session watchdog through the same active-run abort path used for user/restart/superseded aborts. That marked the attempt as `externalAbort=true`, so embedded-run failover surfaced `Request was aborted` even when fallback was configured.
- Solution: Preserve the stuck-session recovery abort reason through the embedded run registry and classify `stuck_recovery` inside the active attempt as an internal idle timeout, not an external abort.
- What changed: Added an explicit embedded abort reason, plumbed `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"` from diagnostic stuck-session recovery through `abortAndDrainEmbeddedAgentRun`, and mapped that reason to `idleTimedOut=true` plus timeout abort semantics in the attempt.
- What did NOT change: No provider catalog changes, no OpenAI-compatible stream parser changes, no opencode-go payload wrapper changes, no fallback policy rewrite, and no gateway protocol/config changes.
- Why it matters / User impact: Cron/provider sessions that have already stopped making model-stream progress can now reuse the existing idle-timeout rotate/fallback path instead of failing as if the user externally aborted the run.
- Fixes #93610

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Stuck diagnostic recovery for an active embedded run now sends a distinct `stuck_recovery` abort reason, and the embedded attempt treats that abort as an internal idle timeout so it no longer trips the `externalAbort` failover short-circuit.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw issue worktree `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-93610` at HEAD `d8c1a3082bf78f0a3fde6334849f4cb1c54c9b84` on Linux with frozen pnpm dependencies installed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 28.09s
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests 31 passed (31)
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests 22 passed (22)
Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests 28 passed (28)

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
```

Evidence logs:

```text
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-93610-evidence/vitest-stuck-recovery-current-head.log
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-93610-evidence/typecheck-diffcheck-current-head.log
/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-93610-evidence/verification-marker.json
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The registry regression proves `abortAndDrainEmbeddedAgentRun` forwards `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"`; the diagnostic recovery regression proves stuck-session recovery supplies that abort reason; the existing failover-policy regression proves non-external idle timeouts with fallback configured escalate to `fallback_model` after profile rotation is exhausted while external aborts still surface.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash provider stream or a live cron job against opencode.ai. The proof is local source/runtime coverage of the abort-classification boundary that produced `externalAbort=true` in the issue trace.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** N/A — new issue flow, no review findings on this PR yet.

## Regression Test Plan
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts`, `src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A stale active embedded run recovered by diagnostics now carries `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"` through the registry; the attempt maps that reason to idle-timeout semantics, and failover keeps the existing non-external timeout fallback behavior.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The bug was not in SSE parsing or opencode-go model config; it was at the abort reason boundary between diagnostic recovery and embedded attempt failover. These tests cover that boundary without requiring live provider credentials.

## Merge risk
- **Risk labels considered:** availability / automation / session-state.
- **Risk explanation:** The change touches active embedded-run abort classification. Incorrect classification could affect session cleanup, user aborts, restart aborts, or timeout retry/fallback behavior.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff preserves user/restart/superseded abort behavior, only assigns timeout semantics to the diagnostic-only `stuck_recovery` reason, keeps force-clear cleanup unchanged, and is covered by registry, diagnostic recovery, failover policy, type, and diff checks.

## Root Cause
- **Root cause:** The root/source defect was an abort-classification contract violation between diagnostic stuck-session recovery and embedded attempt failover. `recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` knew it was reclaiming a stale internal run (`reason: "stuck_recovery"`), but `abortAndDrainEmbeddedAgentRun` discarded that reason on the normal abort path and called `abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` with no reason. The active attempt therefore executed the default queue-handle abort path, set `externalAbort=true`, and caused `resolveRunFailoverDecision` to surface the aborted request before the existing idle-timeout fallback path could run.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This fixes the owner boundary where the semantic signal was lost. Diagnostic recovery now passes `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"` through the embedded run registry, and the attempt treats only that diagnostic reason as an internal idle timeout. The existing failover policy remains the source of truth for whether non-external idle timeouts rotate profile, fallback, or surface error.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Focused transport/runner-state fix with regression coverage; no provider-specific special case, no parser fallback, and no broad failover-policy relaxation.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth ownership stays in the embedded run registry (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.ts` / `run-state.ts`), diagnostic recovery (`src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.ts`), and attempt-local timeout flags (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). This changes the abort semantic before failover observes it, not downstream PR/body/log formatting.

## Public contract review

- **Contract surface:** Internal embedded-run abort reason plumbing only.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** No public API, protocol, config, schema, provider catalog, or model default changes. Existing `user_abort`, `restart`, and `superseded` semantics remain external aborts.
- **Related open PR / competition scan:** Current submission recheck found linked open PR #93640. That PR adds an OpenAI transport guard that only fires after a chunk with `finish_reason`; this submission fixes the failover boundary explicitly shown in the issue trace (`model.completed` with `aborted=true`, `externalAbort=true`, `fallbackConfigured=true`, `decision=surface_error`). The gap matters because the issue impact is not just a missing stream terminator: once diagnostic stuck-session recovery aborts the active embedded run, the registry loses the internal `stuck_recovery` reason and the attempt is classified as an external abort, preventing the existing fallback policy from running. This PR preserves that semantic reason and keeps the fix provider-agnostic, without changing OpenAI stream parsing or opencode-go model configuration.
- **Out of scope:** Live provider transport behavior, opencode-go SSE parsing, fallback model selection policy, and cron scheduler timeout policy are intentionally unchanged.
